wpSpineCondition.title: Preventive Spine Health Consultation
wpSpineCondition.content:
Maintaining spine health is an important part of overall well-being. Many spine conditions develop gradually over time due to factors such as poor posture, sedentary lifestyle, repetitive strain, or age-related changes. 

Preventive spine consultations focus on identifying early signs of spinal stress and helping patients adopt habits that support long-term spinal health. 

Factors that commonly affect spine health include: 

  • Prolonged sitting or desk work
  • Poor posture while working or using devices
  • Lack of physical activity
  • Improper lifting techniques
  • Weak core and back muscles

Preventive spine care may involve: 

  • Posture assessment and correction
  • Ergonomic guidance for workplace setup
  • Strengthening exercises for back and core muscles
  • Advice on safe lifting and movement habits
  • Early evaluation of back or neck discomfort

Addressing small problems early can often prevent the development of more serious spine conditions later. 

Preventive consultations aim to help individuals maintain spinal flexibility, strength, and balance so they can continue daily activities with reduced risk of injury or chronic pain.
